matches.match_rationale: The core problem is that site-level enrollment forecasts are disconnected from corporate targets — a classic demand forecasting failure. Time series models trained on historical intake data (by site, cohort, program, season) can generate a shared, methodology-consistent forecast baseline that both site heads and corporate planners work from, eliminating assumption divergence. This gives the corporate office a defensible, data-driven number to anchor budget and staffing decisions.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A multi-site enrollment forecasting model that ingests historical intake, application pipeline, and demographic signals per campus to produce rolling 12-month enrollment projections — surfaced in a shared dashboard visible to both site heads and corporate leadership.

matches.match_rationale: A major driver of misalignment is that site-level forecasts exist in incompatible formats — spreadsheets, local systems, verbal estimates — making aggregation at corporate level impossible without manual rework. AI-assisted ETL can normalize and map heterogeneous site inputs into a canonical forecasting schema, enabling reliable rollup. This removes the structural barrier to reconciliation before any modeling even begins.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated ingestion layer that pulls forecast submissions from each site (regardless of format), standardizes them against a corporate schema, flags gaps and outliers, and feeds a consolidated corporate view — replacing the current ad hoc aggregation.

matches.match_rationale: When site forecasts are submitted, anomaly detection can automatically flag sites whose projections deviate significantly from historical patterns, peer-site benchmarks, or the corporate model — surfacing misalignment before it becomes a budget surprise. This gives corporate leadership early warning of outlier assumptions without requiring manual review of every site submission.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated forecast review tool that scores each site's intake submission against expected ranges and raises alerts when a site's assumptions diverge materially from the corporate model or comparable sites, prompting reconciliation conversations proactively.

matches.match_rationale: The reconciliation process itself — collecting site forecasts, comparing against corporate targets, generating variance reports, routing discrepancies for review — is a multi-step workflow currently done manually or not at all. A task automation agent can orchestrate this cycle: pulling site submissions, running comparisons, generating reconciliation summaries, and distributing them to relevant stakeholders on a defined cadence. This transforms an ad hoc, error-prone process into a reliable governance loop.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A recurring forecasting reconciliation agent that runs monthly: collects site forecast inputs, diffs them against the corporate model, generates a structured variance report per site, and distributes it with escalation flags to finance and operations leadership.

matches.match_rationale: Once forecast variances are identified, leadership still needs narrative explanations to accompany budget submissions and board reporting. AI text generation can produce plain-language commentary on enrollment forecast variances — explaining which sites are above/below target, likely causes, and implications for staffing and budget — saving finance teams significant time in preparing board-ready materials.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generated enrollment forecast narratives for board packs and budget submissions: structured variance data in, human-readable executive summary out, with site-level callouts and YoY trend commentary.
